question_title: Will Arizona Senator Mark Kelly, who urged troops to defy "illegal" orders, be recalled to active duty in the US military before April 1, 2026?
summary: The Pentagon explicitly chose NOT to recall Kelly to active duty. In January 2026, Defense Secretary Hegseth announced a censure letter and retirement grade review (rank reduction + pay cut) instead of recall - described as "stopping well short" of the recall threat. Kelly has filed a federal lawsuit challenging even these lesser measures, and a federal judge appeared sympathetic at a Feb 3 hearing with a ruling expected by Feb 11. Legal experts broadly agree Kelly did nothing wrong. The community prediction has been at 1% since early January. With active litigation blocking even the lesser measures and only ~7 weeks remaining, recall to active duty is nearly impossible.

factors.description: Pentagon explicitly chose censure + rank review OVER recall to active duty (Jan 5, 2026)
Kelly filed federal lawsuit and judge appears likely to rule in his favor by Feb 11
Legal experts broadly agree Kelly did nothing wrong; case against him is weak
Recalling a sitting US Senator to active duty would be constitutionally extraordinary and unprecedented
Only ~7 weeks remain before resolution deadline
Trump administration could escalate despite having backed down (tail risk)

resolution_reason: Senator Mark Kelly has NOT been placed on active duty status in the United States Armed Forces before April 1, 2026. While the Pentagon threatened to recall him to active duty in November 2025 following a video urging troops to refuse illegal orders, and subsequently opened a formal command investigation, issued a letter of censure, and initiated proceedings to downgrade his retirement rank/pay, Kelly was never actually placed on active duty. A federal judge blocked the Pentagon's actions in January 2026, ruling they violated Kelly's First Amendment rights. The DOJ has appealed, with oral arguments scheduled for May 7, 2026 - after the resolution deadline. Recent news from late March 2026 shows Kelly actively serving as a Senator, participating in Armed Services Committee hearings, indicating he remains in his civilian/retired status.
resolution_criteria: This question resolves as **Yes** if, before April 1, 2026, according to [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions) Senator Mark Kelly has been placed on Active Duty status in the [United States Armed Forces](https://www.war.gov/About/Our-Forces/).
fine_print:

This question will resolve based on the resolution criteria and fine print contained above. However, if this question would resolve differently than the original question, then this question will be annulled. Additionally, if the original question's resolution could have been known before this question opened, then this question will be annulled.
